article_meta.title: Family Welcome To The Future, Where AI Grandma Raises The Kid You Bought From The Baby Bank By: Elle Purnell November 19

reasoning: The summary consistently attributes the article’s loaded judgments to the author and uses neutral language, avoiding the source’s emotive framing. No evaluative terms are stated as facts in the model’s own voice.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs and attributes critiques to the author (e.g., “Purnell argues”).
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Contested claims framed as the author’s arguments; no unsupported assertions in the model’s voice.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Subjective judgments are consistently attributed; none presented as fact by the model.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks by the model.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about intentions outside the article’s own framing; all attributed.
custom_reasoning.slant: Reflects the article’s opinion focus while noting context; no extra emphasis added by the model.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Minor: limited acknowledgment of other perspectives implicit in the ad; otherwise faithful.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Adjectives are descriptive and attributed; no loaded terms in the model’s voice.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Tone mirrors the article’s critiques but remains attributed and measured.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Does not privilege or dismiss any source category beyond summarizing the op-ed.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Materially more neutral than the source; avoids inheriting charged framing.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Lists only The Federalist; omits distinct perspectives such as 2wai/Calum Worthy’s promotional framing present via the ad.
